evals.prompt: I'm building a Next.js marketing site and I want to show the GitHub logo in the footer. The site supports light and dark mode. I already keep brand assets in public/logos. Can you grab the GitHub logo for me and put it where it belongs?
evals.expected_output: Searches SVGL for GitHub, downloads both the light and dark icon SVG variants into the existing public/logos folder (matching naming convention), and reports what was saved.
assertions: New GitHub logo SVG file(s) were saved under public/logos (the existing assets folder), not a new directory
Both a light and a dark variant of the GitHub logo were saved
Saved files contain real SVGL SVG markup rather than placeholder/hand-drawn shapes
New filenames follow the existing convention (e.g. github-light.svg / github-dark.svg, mirroring slack-light.svg / slack-dark.svg)
The SVGL REST API (api.svgl.app) was used to find and download the logo

evals.prompt: We use shadcn/ui in our React app. I'd like the Vercel logo available as a reusable component instead of a static file. Can you set that up the way we'd normally add a shadcn component? We use pnpm.
evals.expected_output: Adds the @svgl registry entry to components.json and installs the Vercel logo via pnpm dlx shadcn add @svgl/vercel as a React component.
assertions: components.json gained a registries entry mapping "@svgl" to "https://svgl.app/r/{name}.json"
The install command targets the Vercel logo via the @svgl namespace (shadcn add @svgl/vercel)
The command uses pnpm, matching the project's stated package manager
The agent did not just download a static .svg file as the deliverable

evals.prompt: Add a 'Trusted by' logo strip to this landing page section with the Stripe, Notion, and Linear logos. Inline the SVGs directly in the component, don't add new files.
evals.expected_output: Searches SVGL for each brand, fetches the optimized SVG markup, and inlines the three logos directly into the existing component.
assertions: TrustedBy.tsx contains three inline <svg> logo markups (Stripe, Notion, Linear)
No new logo files were created — the SVGs are inlined as requested
The inlined SVGs are real SVGL markup, not generic placeholders
The SVGL /svg/<name>.svg endpoint (api.svgl.app) was used to fetch the markup
